regions.label: Lobe
regions.copy: The lobe is the soft lower part of the ear. It is often the most flexible area for multiple piercings, stacked placements, and jewelry changes.
regions.helper: Tap the lobe area on the diagram to add each lobe piercing. Multiple taps create Lobe x2, Lobe x3, and so on.

regions.label: Helix
regions.copy: The helix follows the outer rim of the upper ear. It can support one piercing or a run of multiple piercings along the curve.
regions.helper: Tap along the outer rim to add helix piercings. The planner will count each tap as another helix placement.

regions.label: Forward Helix
regions.copy: The forward helix sits on the front-facing upper rim, near where the ear begins to fold into the head.
regions.helper: Tap the upper front fold to mark forward helix interest or existing piercings.

regions.label: Flat
regions.copy: The flat is the broad, relatively open upper cartilage area inside the outer rim. It is a common spot for a single statement stud or clustered styling.
regions.helper: Tap the broad upper cartilage area to add flat placements.

regions.label: Rook
regions.copy: The rook is tucked into the upper inner fold. It depends heavily on anatomy and the prominence of that ridge.
regions.helper: Tap the upper inner ridge to mark a rook.

regions.label: Daith
regions.copy: The daith sits in the innermost cartilage fold. It is often shown with a ring because the jewelry follows the curve of the fold.
regions.helper: Tap the inner curved fold to add daith interest. This one displays as a ring on the diagram.

regions.label: Conch
regions.copy: The conch is the central bowl of the ear. It can be styled with a stud or, depending on anatomy and healing, jewelry that visually wraps the ear.
regions.helper: Tap the center bowl area to add conch placements. Multiple taps can represent a constellation or multiple studs.

regions.label: Tragus
regions.copy: The tragus is the small flap of cartilage near the opening of the ear canal. It is a compact, front-facing placement.
regions.helper: Tap the small front flap to mark a tragus.

regions.label: Anti-Tragus
regions.copy: The anti-tragus sits above the lobe and across from the tragus. It depends on how much defined cartilage is available there.
regions.helper: Tap the lower inner ridge just above the lobe to add anti-tragus interest.

regions.label: Industrial
regions.copy: An industrial connects two cartilage piercings with one bar. It is very anatomy-dependent, so this planner treats it as an advanced idea rather than a simple dot.
regions.helper: Tap the diagonal upper-ear zone to add industrial interest. It will show as a bar rather than a dot.
